creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to dennis from comment #2)

> >My first guess is that gnucash is using some middleware software to get >information from the card.
> 
> OK is there a chance to get a Logfile
> maybe I can find more about the Problem there ?

look at the output of "gnucash --help" it has a --debug, --log and --logto options you can use to get more detailed logging and choosse where it writes the log to. Maybe the output of that can really help.

As I Said I'm not using such a setup, so I don't know the details. In fact I'm not aware of any banks in my country providing such service(But I did not look that hard I admit).

Looking at gnucash documentation [1] I'd ask you, if you have not already done that, to try performing the setup procedure from scratch.

Have you tried recompiling/reinstalling the ports I suggested in comment 1?

The most probable cause IMHO is one of those packages got out of sync with installed libraries and is erroring out. usually realigning packages fixes this kind of things.

> 
> >You can also try downgrading the gnucash port to 2.6.21 if that proves to be the >actual problem, but from what you tell me it does not look like that is the >actual cause.
> 
> in the packages is GNUCASG 2.6.x but
> not with AqBanking support. So I cant uses this.
> 
> In the Port tree is Version 3.1

I understand, no problem.

BTW I recently added AQBANKING to the default options in the ports head, it will be available in quarterly branch ports in July.

If other options are needed in the package to have such a setup work, please let me know so I can evaluate adding them to the port.



[1] https://wiki.gnucash.org/wiki/Setting_up_OFXDirectConnect_in_GnuCash_2
